'''Antony Smith''' (born 16 July 1970), also known by the [[nickname]] '''"Casper"''', is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s, and [[Coach (sport)|coach]]ed in the 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]], and at club level for the [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Wigan Warriors]] and [[Hull FC]], as a {{rlp|so|sh}} or {{rlp|hk}},<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=230|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|publisher=thecastlefordtigers.co.uk |date=31 December 2013|accessdate=1 January 2014|url-status=unfit|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120216084001/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=230|archivedate=16 February 2012}}</ref> and coached at club level for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].<ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
While at Wigan Smith won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]] against France (sub), Fiji, South Africa (sub), Wales, and Australia. Smith was selected to play for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup|1995 World Cup]] Final at {{rlp|so}} but [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] won the match and retained the Cup.

He again played for England in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]] against Australia, Fiji, Ireland (sub), and New Zealand, and won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] while at Castleford in 1996 against Papua New Guinea (sub), and Fiji (sub), and while at Wigan in 1998 against New Zealand (3 matches).

Tony Smith was a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|so}} [[Grant Anderson (rugby league, born 1969)|Grant Anderson]] at [[half-time]] (40-minutes)) in [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]'s 12–28 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1992 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 2 May 1992, in front of a crowd of 77,386.<ref name="2nd May 1992: Wigan 28 Castleford 12 (Challenge Cup Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=3066|title=2nd May 1992: Wigan 28 Castleford 12 (Challenge Cup Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2016|accessdate=1 January 2017|archive-date=15 February 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170215030604/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=3066|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Tony Smith played, and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]'s 28–6 victory over [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] in the [[1991 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Sunday 20 October 1991.

Tony Smith played for Wigan at {{rlp|sh}} in their [[1998 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos]]. He played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] at {{rlp|so}}, scoring a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in their [[2000 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].

==Coaching career==
Tony Smith is a former coach of [[Super League]] club [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]. He was appointed in August 2005 after a successful spell as caretaker coach following the sacking of Australian [[Shane McNally]]. However, with the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] facing relegation, he was sacked in July 2006 and replaced a few days later by [[John Kear]]. He was subsequently Assistant Coach at [[Featherstone Rovers]] under head coach [[David Hobbs (rugby league)|David Hobbs]].{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}
